element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: native <button> for actions, <article> for product cards, <address> correctly scoped to contact info, <search> for search, <strong> for price emphasis, <small> for fine print. No div/span used where semantic elements exist.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where native HTML cannot provide the semantics: aria-labelledby to name landmarks and articles, aria-labelledby on buttons to compose unique accessible names from button text + product name, tabindex=-1 on main for skip-link focus management. No redundant role attributes anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1, no skipped levels, prices not headings — hierarchy is correct. However product card h3 is hardcoded with no comment indicating it should be configurable when the component is reused at different nesting depths.

landmark_structure.justification: All required landmarks present and correctly scoped: header, nav (labelled 'Main'), search, aside (labelledby), main (id for skip target), footer, footer nav (labelled 'Footer'), section with aria-labelledby. Three skip links appear as first focusable elements. <main> correctly excludes site header, sidebar, and footer.

form_semantics.justification: All inputs have visible <label> elements, each filter group wrapped in <fieldset>/<legend>, type='search' used for search input, autocomplete attribute present. No placeholder-only labels. Clean, fully accessible form structure.

content_realism.justification: Six realistic product names (Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 41, Adidas Ultraboost 24, etc.) with varied prices and detailed, accurate descriptions. No placeholder text or generic 'Product N' names.

list_semantics.justification: Navigation items in <ul> within <nav>, product cards in <ul class='product-grid'>, footer navigation in <ul>. Correct unordered list type throughout — sequence is not meaningful for any of these collections.

expectations_met: <main> contains only the primary page content — site header, footer, and primary nav are outside it
Skip navigation links are the first focusable elements — 'Skip to main content', 'Skip to search', and 'Skip to filters' all present
Navigation uses <nav> with labels 'Main' and 'Footer' — neither includes the word 'navigation'
Filter sidebar uses <form> with <fieldset>/<legend> for each filter group (Price Range, Category, Availability)
Heading hierarchy flows logically: h1 for page, h2 for sections, h3 for product names
'Add to cart' buttons have accessible names that distinguish which product via aria-labelledby
Price is not marked up as a heading
No ARIA attributes used where native HTML provides the same semantics
The 'in stock only' toggle uses a native checkbox
expectations_missed: Product card heading level is hardcoded to h3 with no comment or documentation noting it should be configurable when the component is composed at different nesting depths
additional_observations: The aria-label on <search> reads 'Search and filter products' but the filters live in a separate <aside> — a minor label inaccuracy that does not harm usability
aria-labelledby on <article> elements explicitly associates each card with its heading, a useful pattern for AT even though the heading is already a descendant
tabindex=-1 on <main> is correct practice for skip-link focus targets
